claim: The human edge has shifted from thinking well (now commoditized) to the sound call made before the evidence becomes consensus.
notes: Post #1's specific forward claim (the proposition that 'cleared'), not the whole worldview. graded_by=stress-test (model-checked + logic stress-tested); bump to 'panel' only if the five-lens panel was pointed at this claim.

claim: To improve personal/persuasive writing, excavate the lived kernel and rebuild on a spine before editing prose (the high-value, non-default move).
notes: Ablation isolated the kernel move; held 5/5 on fresh drafts under a cross-model judge. Identified for instance, generalized to class. Mechanism: model does not do it by default.

claim: Accountability talk backfires; build purpose-rooted ownership instead.
notes: Quieter survivor under the Pile-2 headline. Direction plausible, mechanism unproven per skill caveat — consistent-with, not identified.

claim: Bias to impact, not action — aim effort at the outcome via the most direct causal path.
Adding engineers to a late project makes it later.
notes: Model rates it confidently with or without attribution.

claim: Accountability is perfectly anti-correlated with results.
notes: Overconfident headline; graded false. Quieter survivor moved to Pile 3 (see ownership-over-accountability).

claim: Hiring is the single most powerful lever.
notes: Overconfident absolute; one strong lever among several. SOURCE ATTRIBUTION TO VERIFY.
